figures.caption: Figure 3: Performance and efficiency across trajectory lengths. (a) ACGM maintains high retrieval quality (nDCG@10 >79%) up to 𝑇=100, with the gap over baselines widening as history grows. (b) Sub-linear 𝑂 (log𝑇 ) latency (14.7ms at 𝑇=100) vs. linear scaling for dense retrieval methods. (c) Learned modality-specific decay rates (𝜆𝑣=0.47, 𝜆𝑘=0.23, 𝜆𝑥=0.11) capture heterogeneous temporal dynamics, outperforming uniform decay by 8.7 Prec@10 points.
Table 1: Retrieval performance across three benchmarks. Best , Second , Third . * denotes 𝑝 < 0.001 vs. second-best (Bonferronicorrected).

Figure 2: Retrieval analysis, ablations, and decay sensitivity. (a) PR curves (AUC: ACGM 0.863 vs. GPT-4o 0.815) show consistently higher precision. (b) Retrieval quality strongly correlates with task success (𝜌=0.93, 𝑅2=0.991). (c) Ablations across graph topology (RQ3), decay (RQ2), modality (Mod), and retrieval (RQ1) identify learned edges, no-decay, and dense graphs as most critical (Δ>10). (d) Decay sensitivity shows >80 nDCG@10 within ±0.10 of optimum, indicating robustness.
